observation: Stigmergy (pheromone substrate) beats RPC at scale: each Scribe emits topic-tags at write time; queries read the index without polling individual Scribes. Cost is O(1) per query vs O(N) RPC. Real-world analog: search engine inverted index. Google does not poll websites at query time. The Cathedral graduates from 'panel that can be cross-examined' (Detective) to 'self-organizing trail system' (Pheromone).

B128 PoC found 12 scribe JSONL files vs 14 registered. K523 resolves: PoC scanned only scribes/ (12 files). Production build scans 3 Cathedral dirs (scribes/, knight_cathedral/scribes/, pawn_cathedral/scribes/) = 21 total JSONL files. The 2 'missing' are Conductor (empty file, zero tablets written) and then R12Cranewell + R12Covenant (no tablet file = never had scribe_log called). All 14 registered Scribes are accounted for; zero tablets is correct behavior for unwritten Scribes.

Production build (K523) vs PoC (B128): 21 scribe files (vs 12), 1,107 tablets (vs 345), 1,105 records (vs 345), 6,910 distinct topics (vs 4,841). Knight + Pawn Cathedral dirs add ~760 tablets not in the PoC scope. Build time: 153ms. The ~10^6 speedup claim from the PoC holds at production scale -- query_ms is still 0-1ms.

Two Scribe write paths hooked with sync pheromone emit: appendScribeEntry (called by scribe_log MCP tool) emits to bishop Cathedral; appendTidbit (called by log_tidbit MCP tool) emits to virtual 'Tidbits' scribe. Both hooks are non-fatal (try/catch wrapping) -- pheromone emit must NEVER break the underlying Scribe write. Target <5ms sync-emit; slow emits logged to stderr for tuning.

Exponential decay formula: decay_score = match_strength * exp(-age_days / decay_constant_days). Default decay_constant_days = 30. A 100-day-old tablet with match_strength=3 scores 0.14 vs a 1-day-old tablet of same match_strength scoring 3.87 (ratio ~27x). This naturally surfaces recent content without deleting old -- old content preserved for forensic queries (decay_active=false).

Primary key for deduplication: (cathedral, scribe, tablet_id). Re-emitting same key overwrites the old record in the in-memory Map and appends a new line to the JSONL. On disk: the JSONL accumulates duplicates but the in-memory index deduplciates (last-write-wins). Full rebuild (Bloodhound) produces clean deduplicated JSONL. The (cathedral, scribe, tablet_id) key is stable for the same observation entry -- repeated calls to appendScribeEntry for the same session entry produce deterministic tablet_id via line_count suffix.

detective_investigate MCP tool wires Phase 0 (pheromone) before Phase 1 (RPC). Phase 0: pheromone_query returns ranked hits + phase_0_used flag. Phase 1: consult_scribes RPC runs only when phase_0_used=false (sparse index). Provenance source tagged: 'pheromone_substrate' (fast path) or 'rpc_consult_scribes' (RPC fallback). Routine 'where does X live?' queries hit Phase 0 at <5ms; novel queries fall through to Phase 1.

extractTopics() required explicit hyphenated-term regex /\b([a-z]{3,}(?:-[a-z]{3,})+)\b/g to capture 'append-only', 'cross-vendor', 'decay-constant', etc. The single-token regex [a-z][a-z_]{3,30} only allows [a-z_] chars -- hyphens break word boundaries. Without the hyphenated regex, compound technical terms would be split into their component words ('append' + 'only') losing the specific compound meaning. Added in K523 Phase B.

Bloodhound (pheromone-bloodhound.mjs) runs deeper extraction than sync-emit: adds CamelCase identifier pattern, hyphenated terms, K/B/R/P session ID pattern, more quoted phrases. Respects decay: tablets older than 150 days (decay factor < 0.01) get only forensic indexing (scribe name + session ID). Designed for nightly/hourly cron via 'npm run pheromone:build'. Wired into npm run rebuild so every Librarian rebuild refreshes the pheromone index.

librarian-mcp/.gitignore line 25 already gitignores stitchpunks/pheromone_substrate/ -- confirmed at K523 step A.0a. The index is a derived artifact (rebuild any time from source Scribe tablets) so gitignoring is correct. No new .gitignore entries needed.

The production pheromone index covers 3 Cathedrals: bishop (11 non-empty Scribes), knight (5 Scribes: KnightArchitecture/BRIDLE/Handoffs/Queue/R11), pawn (4 Scribes: PawnGenerated/Handoffs/Queue/R11_corpus). Cross-Cathedral queries filter by cathedral field. Claim 7 (Hound-propagated pheromone-summaries between Cathedrals) is the roadmap item; K523 lays the foundation by including all Cathedrals in the index.

Claim 9 of #2317: pheromone substrate adds 5th layer to compounding-savings algorithm. Pre-#2317: ~26x multiplier (B127 baseline). Post-#2317: ~26x * 800,000 = ~2*10^7. At 80% pheromone hit-rate, Investigation-cost-collapse layer = (0.8 * 10^6 + 0.2 * 1) ~= 800,001. The 'Penny Saved §5.4 99% cheaper' public claim becomes a structural under-claim by orders of magnitude. G.5 empirical confirmation: production index query returned 20 hits in 1ms wall-clock vs ~30 min Bishop manual Detective sweep = confirmed ~10^6 speedup.

Three new MCP tools registered: (1) pheromone_query -- Detective Phase 0 fast path, returns ranked hits + timing + phase_0_used flag; (2) pheromone_build -- force full index rebuild (expensive but fast, <200ms); (3) detective_investigate -- unified Detective interface: Phase 0 pheromone check, Phase 1 consult_scribes RPC fallback, structured Provenance Map output. pheromone_build also wired into npm run rebuild so every Librarian rebuild auto-refreshes.
